2026-04-22T19:06:58.3520479Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T19:06:58.3521230Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T19:06:58.3522103Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T19:06:58.3522634Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:06:58.3523041Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:06:58.3523312Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m

2026-04-22T19:11:55.4043052Z   1. **`PlatformHeaders` not in Key Concepts** — This is a new public class that centralizes all HTTP header name constants. It's important for developers using the SDK to know these constants exist so they don't hardcode header names.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.4044566Z   
2026-04-22T19:11:55.4044808Z

2026-04-22T19:11:55.4049903Z   3. **Getting Started section is missing the Tier 3 standalone setup** — The README covers `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` but doesn't show how to configure the server independently using the new methods.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.4051673Z   4. **Samples directory is nearly empty** — The `samples/README.md` exists but lacks practical examples.

CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-22T19:11:55.5946052Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T19:11:41.132699601Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58396) by @RaviPidaparthi\n\n## What Changed\n\nPR #58396 added two significant new public APIs in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.23:\n\n1. **`PlatformHeaders` static class** — Centralizes all cross-protocol HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). This is a new **public** type visible in the API listing.\n2. **`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` extension methods** — Replaced six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId`, `AddAgentServerVersion`, `AddAgentServerLogging`, and their `Use*` counterparts) with a single pair. This is documented as a breaking change in CHANGELOG.md, but the README has no code example showing this Tier 3 standalone setup pattern.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a public class in the API listing (`api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.net8.0.cs`) but is absent from the README's **Key concepts** section — developers building custom middleware or handlers have no guidance that these constants exist.\n- The `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is only mentioned in a single sentence in the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84 of README); there is no code snippet showing how to wire up the Tier 3 standalone setup, making the breaking change from 6 methods to 2 invisible to new readers.\n- The Getting Started section has no \"Standalone / Tier 3 setup\" sub-section showing `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` usage alongside the existing `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` (Tier 1/2) example.\n- No sample test file backing a `PlatformHeaders` or Tier 3 snippet exists under `tests/Snippets/`.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit\n\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the \"Health endpoint\" subsection), add a new `### PlatformHeaders` subsection:\n\n```markdown\n### PlatformHeaders\n\nA static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages. Refer to these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings in custom middleware or handlers:\n\n| Constant | Header | Direction |\n|---|---|---|\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request \u0026 response — correlation ID |\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response — SDK identity |\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response — resolved session ID |\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request — user partition key |\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request — conversation partition key |\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request — W3C trace context propagation |\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request — Azure SDK correlation |\n```\n\n2. In the **Getting started** section, add a new `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section **after** the existing `### Start a server (recommended)` sub-section. The new sub-section should explain when to use this pattern and include a snippet placeholder:\n\n```markdown\n### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\n\nIf you are integrating AgentServer middleware into an existing ASP.NET Core application rather than using `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`, call `AddAgentServerCore()` during service registration and `UseAgentServerCore()` during middleware configuration:\n\n```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\n// In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);\nbuilder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\n\nvar app = builder.Build();\napp.UseAgentServerCore();\n// ... map your protocol routes ...\napp.Run();\n```\n\nThis registers the `x-request-id` response header middleware, the server version (`x-platform-server`) header middleware, and inbound request logging — the same middleware that `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` enables automatically.\n```\n\n3. Update the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84) to reference the new Getting Started sub-section instead of re-explaining the methods inline.\n\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`** — Edit\n\nAdd a new test method backing the `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` snippet:\n\n```csharp\n[Test]\n[Explicit(\"Snippets are compiled to prevent rot but require a running server to execute.\")]\npublic void Tier3StandaloneSetup()\n{\n    #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\n\n    // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\n    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args: []);\n    builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\n\n    var app = builder.Build();\n    app.UseAgentServerCore();\n    // ... map your protocol routes ...\n    app.Run();\n\n    #endregion\n}\n```\n\nAlso add the required `using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;` import if not already present.\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\n1. **Test file path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`\n   - Add method `Tier3StandaloneSetup` with `#region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` / `#endregion`\n   - Any test-only assertions must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\n\n2. **README placeholder:** In `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`, under the new \"### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\" sub-section, insert:\n   ````\n   ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\n   ```\n   ````\n   The snippet name is `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` — must match exactly between the test region and the README placeholder.\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already has the required top-level sections. The following updates are needed:\n\n- **Getting started** — Add `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section *(missing)*\n- **Key concepts** — Add `### PlatformHeaders` sub-section *(missing)*\n- All other required sections (Prerequisites, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present.\n\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\n\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the Tier 3 snippet\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public type changes expected; verify API listing is unchanged\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and AddAgentServerCore/UseAgentServerCore Tier 3 pattern in Core README"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-22T19:11:55.6313474Z server:unified callBackendTool: serverID=safeoutputs, toolName=create_issue, args=map[body:## Documentation Gap
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6313481Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6313577Z **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6313746Z **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6313994Z **Triggered by:** commit 685f2f019a1387dac852b731ef7b9e4632eeacb0 (PR #58396) by @RaviPidaparthi
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6314005Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6314076Z ## What Changed
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6314080Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6314314Z PR #58396 added two significant new public APIs in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.23:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6314318Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6315284Z 1. **`PlatformHeaders` static class** — Centralizes all cross-protocol HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). This is a new **public** type visible in the API listing.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6316794Z 2. **`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` extension methods** — Replaced six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId`, `AddAgentServerVersion`, `AddAgentServerLogging`, and their `Use*` counterparts) with a single pair. This is documented as a breaking change in CHANGELOG.md, but the README has no code example showing this Tier 3 standalone setup pattern.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6316804Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6316882Z ## Gaps Found
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6316886Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6317872Z - `PlatformHeaders` is a public class in the API listing (`api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.net8.0.cs`) but is absent from the README's **Key concepts** section — developers building custom middleware or handlers have no guidance that these constants exist.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6318739Z - The `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is only mentioned in a single sentence in the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84 of README); there is no code snippet showing how to wire up the Tier 3 standalone setup, making the breaking change from 6 methods to 2 invisible to new readers.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319326Z - The Getting Started section has no "Standalone / Tier 3 setup" sub-section showing `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` usage alongside the existing `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` (Tier 1/2) example.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319573Z - No sample test file backing a `PlatformHeaders` or Tier 3 snippet exists under `tests/Snippets/`.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319675Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319750Z <details>
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319946Z <summary><strong>📐 Implementation Guide</strong></summary>
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6319950Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320244Z This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320249Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320323Z ### Step 1: Modify files
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320327Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320554Z **File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320558Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320862Z 1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the "Health endpoint" subsection), add a new `### PlatformHeaders` subsection:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320865Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6320932Z ```markdown
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321004Z ### PlatformHeaders
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321008Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321570Z A static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages. Refer to these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings in custom middleware or handlers:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321580Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321660Z | Constant | Header | Direction |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6321729Z |---|---|---|
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6322005Z | `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request & response — correlation ID |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6322279Z | `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response — SDK identity |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6322546Z | `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response — resolved session ID |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6322881Z | `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request — user partition key |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6323241Z | `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request — conversation partition key |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6323530Z | `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request — W3C trace context propagation |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6323846Z | `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request — Azure SDK correlation |
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6323920Z ```
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6323924Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6324633Z 2. In the **Getting started** section, add a new `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section **after** the existing `### Start a server (recommended)` sub-section. The new sub-section should explain when to use this pattern and include a snippet placeholder:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6324638Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6324705Z ```markdown
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6324784Z ### Standalone (Tier 3) setup
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6324788Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6325526Z If you are integrating AgentServer middleware into an existing ASP.NET Core application rather than using `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`, call `AddAgentServerCore()` during service registration and `UseAgentServerCore()` during middleware configuration:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6325530Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6325741Z ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6325855Z // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6325975Z var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326078Z builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326082Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326158Z var app = builder.Build();
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326349Z app.UseAgentServerCore();
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326438Z // ... map your protocol routes ...
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326503Z app.Run();
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326565Z ```
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6326570Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6327359Z This registers the `x-request-id` response header middleware, the server version (`x-platform-server`) header middleware, and inbound request logging — the same middleware that `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` enables automatically.
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6327422Z ```
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6327425Z 
2026-04-22T19:11:55.6327828Z 3. Update the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84) to reference the new Getting Started sub-section instead of re-explaining the methods inline.
